message:
test(core): wrap in_exists_regression bulk inserts in BEGIN/COMMIT for budget stability

Wrap the 1000-row / 500-row / 100-row bulk-insert sections of three
guard tests in a single transaction so each test's timing budget
measures what it claims to measure (the IN/EXISTS query path) instead
of the per-statement autocommit overhead that the inserts incur without
an explicit txn.

Tests updated:
- guard_large_in_subquery_completes_within_budget
  (lookup: 1000 rows, data: 1000 rows)
- guard_correlated_exists_completes_within_budget
  (parent: 500 rows, child: up to 1500 rows)
- test_direct_probe_exists_simple_correlated
  (customers: 100 rows, orders: ~100 rows)

Each test gets an explicit `conn.execute("BEGIN")` before the first
insert and `conn.execute("COMMIT")` after the last. No other behavior
changes: rows stored are identical, the query-under-budget assertion is
unchanged, the probe logic is untouched.

Without the transaction, the 1000-insert loop alone can eat a
measurable fraction of the per-test budget on slower hosts, making
these tests flake under CI load even when the IN/EXISTS path they
actually guard is healthy. Wrapping in a single transaction eliminates
the insert-side noise and pins the budget to the path we care about.
